data.title: Stoke Therapeutics gets FDA breakthrough therapy designation for the treatment of dravet syndrome
data.description:
• Stoke Therapeutics (NASDAQ:STOK) received Breakthrough Therapy Designation from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for zorevunersen for the treatment of Dravet syndrome with a confirmed mutation, not associated with gain-of-function, in the SCN1A gene.
  • Zorevunersen is being developed as potentially the first disease-modifying medicine for the treatment of Dravet syndrome.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Eli Lilly’s Zepbound outperforms Novo's Wegovy for weight loss in head-to-head trial
data.description:
Update: Adds statement from Novo Nordisk.

Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) said Wednesday its weight-loss drug Zepbound (tirzepatide) provided a 47% greater relative weight loss compared to Danish rival Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) in the first head-to-head trial of the two blockbuster weight loss drugs.

In a study sponsored by Lilly, people taking Zepbound lost an average of 20% of their body weight, while those on Wegovy shed 14%. Specifically, participants using Zepbound lost 50.3 lbs (22.8 kg) and participants on Wegovy lost 33.1 lbs (15.0 kg).

At 72 weeks, Zepbound beat Wegovy on both the primary endpoint and all five key secondary endpoints in this 751-person trial (SURMOUNT-5 phase 3b) of adults living with obesity or overweight with at least one weight-related medical problem and without diabetes.

In a key secondary endpoint, 31.6% of people taking Zepbound achieved at least 25% body weight loss compared to 16.1% of those taking Wegovy.

The full results will be published in a peer-reviewed journal and presented at a medical meeting next year, Lilly said.

Tirzepatide is commercialized for adults with obesity or with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems as Zepbound in the U.S. and Mounjaro in some global markets outside the U.S. The drug is also commercialized as Mounjaro for adults with type 2 diabetes in the U.S.

Semaglutide is commercialized as Wegovy for people living with obesity or for adults with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems and Ozempic for people with type 2 diabetes.

Novo Nordisk told Seeking Alpha: "This is a top-line announcement, and we will await the complete data, including the distribution of treatment doses in the two groups, once the trial has been peer-reviewed and/or published. A greater magnitude of weight loss has previously been shown in the clinical trial program with semaglutide 2.4 mg."

The spokesperson further claimed: "In combination with diet and exercise, Wegovy is the only obesity medicine proven to reduce the risk of major cardiovascular events such as death, heart attack, or stroke in adults with known heart disease and with either obesity or overweight. These significant CV events were assessed regardless of change in weight in the landmark SELECT trial."

U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) moved marginally premarket. Lilly (LLY) shares rose as much as 2% in trading before U.S. markets opened.

data.title: Crescent Energy subsidiary plans additional debt offering
data.description:
Crescent Energy’s (NYSE:CRGY) indirect subsidiary Crescent Energy Finance plans to offer $300M aggregate principal amount of additional 7.625% Senior Notes due 2032 in a private placement.

The notes are being offered as additional notes under the indenture dated as of March 26, 2024, pursuant to which Crescent Energy Finance has previously issued $700M of 7.625% Senior Notes due 2032.

The additional notes will have substantially identical terms, other than the issue date, the first interest payment date and the initial offering price, as the existing notes.

The notes mature on April 1, 2032 and pay interest at the rate of 7.625% per year, payable on April 1 and October 1 of each year, with interest payments on the additional notes commencing on April 1, 2025.

Net proceeds, along with proceeds from the company’s recent stock offering, will be used to fund the cash portion of the consideration for the previously announced acquisition of Ridgemar (Eagle Ford) LLC as well as to reduce the borrowings outstanding under revolving credit facility.

Shares were down 2.51% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Marvell Technology surges as Wall Street praises its 'all in' AI mantra
data.description:
Wall Street firmly believes that the latest results and guidance from Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) are another reason why the artificial intelligence gravy train isn't slowing any time soon.

Shares of the semiconductor company jumped 12% in premarket trading, while competitor Broadcom (AVGO) rose nearly 3%.

"Next year, both custom AI silicon and electro-optics are expected to grow meaningfully, leading to significant upside to our prior estimates," Needham analyst N. Quinn Bolton wrote in a note to clients.

Bolton added that Marvell's AI revenue is now forecast to top the previous $1.5B target by "hundreds of millions of dollars," as the company's two lead customers for custom silicon — believed to be Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) — are ramping up, with Amazon likely driving much of the upside.

"AMZN remains on track to ramp its next-gen custom silicon compute solutions in CY25, and we believe MRVL will be the ASIC partner," Bolton wrote, adding that the company's third customer —believed to be Microsoft (MSFT) — is on track for 2026. "This opportunity is expected to be larger than the engagements with its first two customers," Bolton added. He reiterated his Buy rating and upped his price target to $120 from $95 on Marvell.

Citi analyst Atif Malik maintained his Buy rating and said the company has "hit an inflection point" in sales of custom AI chips, notably Amazon's Trainium2. Amazon said on Tuesday that the Trainium2 is now "generally available" and the Trainium3 will come in 2025.

Malik, who upped his price target to $112 from $91, also positively mentioned Marvell's relationship with Amazon, pointing to the expanded five-year partnership announced earlier this week.

KeyBanc Capital Markets analyst John Vinh also upped his price target — to $125 from $95 — and said he was "encouraged" by the Marvell's results and guidance. He also pointed to the fact that Marvell Chief Executive Matt Murphy said he was "all in" on Marvell, amid speculation he could leave for the top job at Intel (INTC).

Financial results, guidance

For the period ending November 2, Marvell said it earned an adjusted $0.43 per share as revenue rose 6.9% year-over-year to come in at $1.52B. Analysts expected $0.41 per share and $1.45B in sales.

Data center revenue accounted for the vast majority of the $1.52B, as sales from that segment rose 98% year-over-year to come in at $1.1B, ahead of the $1.05B estimate.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, Marvell expects revenue to be $1.8B, plus or minus 5%. Adjusted earnings are forecast to be $0.59 per share, plus or minus $0.05. Analysts were expecting $0.52 per share in adjusted earnings and $1.65B in revenue.

data.title: J.P. Morgan reiterates Overweight rating on HBAN, downgrades M&T Bank
data.description:
J.P. Morgan reiterated its Overweight rating on Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ:HBAN), while downgraded M&T Bank (NYSE:MTB) to Neutral.

For HBAN, the analysts see an upside to 2025 EPS driven by better than expected net interest income (NII) trends.

"There has been some concern about HBAN’s perceived asset sensitivity, and hence concern about weaker NII trends with Federal Reserve's rate cuts," said J.P. Morgan, and added it views these concerns as overdone given its deposit mix and strategy.

The analysts also see the headwind around receive fixed swaps becoming active in 2025 as manageable.

"We forecast upside to 2025 NII despite a more conservative view on loan growth versus the Street...longer term, HBAN should also hold up better if the economy slows given its more conservative credit risk profile," J.P. Morgan said in a note.

J.P. Morgan also raised the target price to $20 from $18.5.

Shares in the company were up 0.8% in premarket trade.

SA analysts rate the company at Buy, while SA quant rates it at Hold.

"We view MTB’s earnings outlook as solid, with large benefit in 2025 from roll off of receive fixed swaps driving sizable uplift to net interest income (NII)," the analysts said.

However, J.P. Morgan expects its office CRE exposure and relatively lower loan loss reserves to remain a headwind.

The analysts said it is moving to the sidelines on MTB as they do not see a catalyst for continued outperformance.

"It is in a strong position to ramp up buybacks in 2025, and should benefit in future stress tests from reduction of CRE exposure," the analysts said.

J.P. Morgan expects office CRE charge-offs to take time to resolve and are more constructive than the Street on the outlook for provisions and net charge-offs.

Shares in the company were down 0.6% in premarket trade.

SA quant rates the company at Strong Buy, while SA analysts rate it at Hold.

data.title: Pure Storage soars after 'robust' Q3, hyperscaler win keep analysts bullish
data.description:
Pure Storage (NYSE:PSTG) stock soared about 24% premarket on Wednesday after fiscal third-quarter results and guidance beat estimates and analysts maintained their bullish views on the company.

Piper Sandler upgraded Pure Storage's rating to Overweight from Neutral and raised the price target on the stock to $76 from $56.

Analysts led by James Fish said that Pure Storage's fiscal third quarter results were better than expected given traditional-sales, but lead metrics struggled.

However, they upgraded the stock, noting that a significant design win with a top-four cloud hyperscaler removes risk and creates a catalyst for upside ahead.

The analysts said that, the opportunity with this hyperscaler alone represents a more than $500M opportunity in 2026, more hyperscalers could be coming, and the narrative shifts to Cloud and AI over STaaS (Storage as a Service). Without this win and given lead-metrics' miss, the stock would have likely been down, the analysts added.

Other reasons for the upgrade, according to the analysts, are — Pure Storage has differentiation, proven by the large software-win; Flash is gaining share, including with cloud-players now around AI use-cases; and the STaaS transition has paused, effectively removing a revenue headwind.

In addition, the analysts see additional estimate upside and room for multiple expansion given the narrative shift.

Wells Fargo Securities kept its Overweight rating on the shares and raised the price target to $80 from $75.

Analysts Aaron Rakers and his team said that Pure Storage's shares are trading significantly higher following the announcement that the company has secured a design win at a top-4 hyperscaler - reinforcing their belief in the company's software-driven architectural differentiation as a key differentiator to such a design win. The company also noted discussions with several other hyperscalers.

The analysts said that Pure Storage expects minimal revenue contributions in 2025; materializing licensing-only (no hardware) revenue into 2026 with double-digits Exabytes ship tied to next-gen data center design/deploy.

J.P. Morgan maintained its Overweight rating and increased the price target on the shares to $75 from $60.

Analysts led by Pinjalim Bora said Pure Storage delivered a robust quarter with fiscal third results landing above expectations. The main highlight of Pure Storage's call was that the company signed a design win with a top four hyperscaler, which means that the hyperscaler will use Pure Storage's technology for all online storage needs, and that all future data centers for this hyperscaler are now approved to use Pure's technology as the standard for data storage, underscoring the company's strong differentiation in the market.

Pure expects early field trial buildouts to start next year, with large full production deployments, on the order of double-digit Exabytes, expected in 2026 (the company's fiscal 2027), with revenue and operating margin expansion to follow at that time, according to the analysts.

Bora and his team think that the stock is likely rising as investors look to digest the implications of the hyperscaler deal, although it will likely take a few quarters to understand the exact scope and size of the deal. Nevertheless, it lays the foundation for what the analysts think could be a very material growth lever for Pure Storage for years to come.

In addition, the analysts said that the deal further validates their medium term thesis that Pure Storage continues to distinguish itself from peers from a product innovation standpoint, especially as it embarks on its goal to expand the capacity of its DirectFlash, or DFM, modules over the next few years.

Related stocks: NetApp (NTAP) +1%, Nutanix (NTNX) +1% premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Aramco, SLB and Linde partner to build huge Saudi carbon capture hub
data.description:
Saudi Aramco (ARMCO), oilfield services company SLB (NYSE:SLB) and industrial gases company Linde (LIN) said Wednesday they signed an agreement to build a carbon capture and storage project at its main refining and chemicals hub in Jubail, Saudi Arabia.

The project, which is expected to become one of the world's largest CCS hubs, will be 60% owned by Aramco (ARMCO), while SLB (SLB) and Linde (LIN) will hold 20% each, the companies said; financial terms were not disclosed.

The project's Phase 1 is expected to be complete by the end of 2027, capturing and storing up to 9M metric tons/year of carbon dioxide.

The Jubail CCS project is considered a key to Aramco's (ARMCO) plans to use a large amount of the natural gas from its giant Jafurah development to produce blue hydrogen and ammonia.

The fuels, which can be exported and used for heat or power, depend on converting natural gas to hydrogen and sequestering the carbon emissions to meet environmental standards.
